{"key_event":"Hezbollah veto power and civic protest amid state paralysis","reasoning":"State power fell as Hezbollah’s armed autonomy, veto politics, institutional vacancies, refugee pressures, and service failures outweighed gains from LAF professionalization, the 2017 budget, and counterterrorism successes. Society power rose modestly: sectarian parties and Hezbollah remained powerful non-state mobilizers, while NGO networks, the garbage protests, and Beirut Madinati expanded cross-sectarian civic capacity despite clientelism limiting accountability.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":6.9,"state_change":-0.5,"state_power":4.2}
